message:
fix(suggest): allow cast_precision_loss for jaccard similarity

Small numeric precision loss is acceptable for similarity scoring.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-p9e","title":"Implement Terraform extractor (provisioners)","description":"## Objective

Implement an extractor for Terraform files to detect executable provisioner contexts.

## File Pattern

- `*.tf`

## Extraction Targets

### Provisioner Blocks (executed)
- `local-exec` command
- `remote-exec` inline commands
- `null_resource` provisioners

### NOT Extracted (data only)
- Variable definitions
- Output values
- Resource attributes (non-provisioner)
- Comments

## Example

```hcl
resource "null_resource" "cleanup" {
  provisioner "local-exec" {
    command = "rm -rf /tmp/*"  # EXTRACT THIS
  }
}

variable "dangerous" {
  default = "rm -rf /"  # DO NOT extract
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- Correctly extracts provisioner commands with line numbers
- Handles HCL2 syntax (Terraform 0.12+)
- Ignores non-executable blocks
- Unit tests with representative fixtures","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### False positive controls
- Extract only from truly executed shell contexts (e.g., `local-exec`, `remote-exec`), not from:
  - documentation strings
  - variable defaults
- Negative fixtures must include data-only mentions of destructive commands.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:24:57.275380054-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T13:11:26.177891816-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-09T13:11:26.177891816-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented Terraform provisioner extractor with local-exec/remote-exec support, HCL2 parsing, heredoc handling. Added to scan flow. 6 tests pass. Committed in a6b640d.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-p9e","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:25:20.549018763-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-p9e","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:46:12.516806682-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-pbte","title":"Cross-cutting UX/Perf/Docs Enablement","description":"# Epic: Cross-cutting UX/Perf/Docs Enablement

## Purpose
Track shared work that supports multiple feature epics (highlighting, explanations,
allowlist suggestions, custom packs) without coupling their delivery.

## Goals
- Config toggles for new UX output
- Explain JSON schema updates when fields are added
- Performance budget checks for new steps
- User-facing documentation updates
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:22:24.351363214-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T00:22:24.351363214-05:00"}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-pbte.1","title":"[Cross] Config toggles for highlighting + explanations","description":"# Task: Config toggles for highlighting + explanations

## Purpose
Allow users to enable/disable new UX output (highlighting, explanations)
without recompiling or forking.

## Requirements
- Config keys with defaults that preserve existing behavior
- Hook output respects toggles
- CLI output respects toggles

## Acceptance Criteria
- Config can independently toggle highlighting and explanations
- Default behavior remains safe and predictable

-{"id":"git_safety_guard-pbte.2","title":"[Cross] Explain JSON schema update + version bump","description":"# Task: Explain JSON schema update + version bump

## Purpose
If new fields are added to explain output (highlight spans, explanations),
update the JSON schema version and documentation.

## Requirements
- Bump `EXPLAIN_JSON_SCHEMA_VERSION`
- Update JSON output struct docs and README
- Ensure backward compatibility notes are explicit

## Acceptance Criteria
- Schema version bump recorded in docs
- `dcg explain --format json` includes new fields with stable names

+{"id":"git_safety_guard-pbte.2","title":"[Cross] Explain JSON schema update + version bump","description":"# Task: Explain JSON schema update + version bump

## Purpose
If new fields are added to explain output (highlight spans, explanations),
update the JSON schema version and documentation.

## Requirements
- Bump `EXPLAIN_JSON_SCHEMA_VERSION`
- Update JSON output struct docs and README
- Ensure backward compatibility notes are explicit

## Acceptance Criteria
- Schema version bump recorded in docs
- `dcg explain --format json` includes new fields with stable names

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-pbte.3","title":"[Cross] Performance budget checks for new steps","description":"# Task: Performance budget checks for new steps

## Purpose
Ensure new formatting and explanation logic does not regress hook performance.

## Requirements
- Identify new steps that should be timed (highlight formatting, explanation lookup)
- Add budget thresholds or confirm existing budgets are sufficient
- Add perf tests or benchmarks if needed

## Acceptance Criteria
- No measurable regression vs existing budget targets
- Budget expectations documented if new steps are added

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-pbte.4","title":"[Cross] Documentation updates for new UX + packs","description":"# Task: Documentation updates for new UX + packs

## Purpose
Keep README and docs aligned with new features:
- Highlighting
- Explanations
- Suggest-allowlist
- Custom pack authoring

## Requirements
- README sections updated with examples
- docs/ pages updated or added for new commands
- Include JSON output examples where applicable

## Acceptance Criteria
- README reflects new user-facing behavior
- Docs include example output and configuration flags

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-pbte.5","title":"[Cross] Unit tests for config toggles (highlighting/explanations)","description":"# Task: Unit tests for config toggles (highlighting/explanations)

## Purpose
Verify that config toggles reliably enable/disable highlighting and explanations
without affecting decision logic.

## Requirements
- Unit tests for config parsing + defaults
- Tests that toggles suppress/enable output sections
- Ensure toggles do not change allow/deny decisions

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests cover both toggles independently and together
- Failures show expected vs actual output sections
